legal.cgu.text:
Terms and Conditions of Use (TCU)

Last updated: April 14, 2026

Article 1: Purpose

The following Terms and Conditions of Use (hereinafter "TCU") define the terms and conditions under which the company Mmedia Universe (hereinafter "the Publisher") provides users (hereinafter "the User") with the Aria OS application (hereinafter "the Application"), as well as the rights and obligations of the parties in this context.

Access to and use of the Application are subject to the acceptance of and compliance with these TCU. By creating an account or using the Application, the User expressly and unreservedly accepts these TCU.

Article 2: Access to the Application

The Application is currently offered free of charge, with the Publisher reserving the right to modify pricing conditions in the future. All costs related to access, whether hardware, software, or internet access fees, are exclusively the responsibility of the User.

Access to certain features requires the creation of a user account via the Google authentication service (Google OAuth). The User agrees to provide accurate information and to maintain the confidentiality of their login credentials.

Article 3: Service Description

Aria OS is a professional tool helping in the creation of editorial content. It notably allows:

  • Analysis of text content, articles, or videos (via URL), etc.
  • Automated generation of documents, such as calibrated scripts for voice-over, via our artificial intelligence.
  • Use of an integrated video recording studio (with teleprompter, take management, video export, etc.).
  • Saving, history tracking of processes, and sharing.
  • Potential display of advertising banners and promotional content in the interface, etc.

Article 4: Intellectual Property and Copyright

4.1. Rights to the Application: The Application's architecture, trademarks, logos, graphics, software, algorithms, and databases are the exclusive property of Mmedia Universe and are protected by intellectual property law. The platform retains all rights to its technology, algorithms, and interface.

4.2. Third-party Trademarks: Trademarks and logos of third parties (such as YouTube, Reuters, etc.) displayed on the Application belong to their respective owners. They are used solely for illustration purposes to indicate the compatibility of our tool with these platforms. Mmedia Universe claims no affiliation or rights to these trademarks.

4.3. Rights in Sources: The User agrees to submit only URLs or texts to the Application for which they have the necessary rights (copyright, right of citation, public domain). The Publisher declines any responsibility in case of analysis of protected content fraudulently submitted by the User.

4.4. Rights in Generated Content: The User has the right to freely, fully, and commercially exploit the scripts generated by the Application. The Publisher claims no copyright on the final texts produced by the User via the Application.

Article 5: Liability and IA Limits

The Application relies on advanced Artificial Intelligence models to analyze provided sources and structure content according to optimized formats for voice-over. Our technology combines these models with exclusive analysis processes to ensure a professional result, without the details of our internal algorithms being public. The User acknowledges that:

  • AI can generate inaccurate, incomplete, or obsolete information (hallucination phenomenon).
  • It is the sole responsibility of the User (as a content creator or journalist) to verify, fact-check, and validate scripts before any publication or recording.

Mmedia Universe shall in no case be held liable for direct or indirect damages resulting from the use of generated scripts (defamation, fake news, commercial prejudice).

Article 6: Service Availability

The Publisher endeavors to keep the Application accessible 24/7 but is bound by no obligation of result. The Publisher reserves the right to suspend, interrupt, or limit access to all or part of the Application for maintenance or update reasons.

Article 7: TCU Modification

Mmedia Universe reserves the right to modify these TCU at any time. Users will be informed of any substantial modification. Continued use of the Application after modification constitutes acceptance of the new TCU.

legal.privacy.text:
Privacy Policy and Cookies

1. Personal Data Collection

Within the use of the Aria OS Application, we collect the following data:

  • Identity Data: First name, last name, email address, and profile picture (provided by Google during OAuth login).
  • Usage Data: History of generated scripts, submitted URLs, analyzed texts, generation parameters (tone, duration).
  • Technical Data: Login identifiers (Firebase UID).

2. Processing Purpose

Your data is processed for the following purposes:

  • Provide the script generation service.
  • Enable saving and consultation of your personal history.
  • Ensure Application security and prevent abuse (quota limitation).
  • Improve our generation algorithms and offerings (anonymized), etc.

3. Data Retention

Your identity data and history are kept as long as your account is active. You have a feature in the Application settings allowing you to permanently delete your account and all your data. Once this action is performed, data is unrecoverable.

4. Data Sharing

Your data is never sold to third parties. It is shared only with our technical subcontractors strictly necessary for the service's operation, and notably:

  • Google Firebase: For database hosting and authentication.
  • Google Gemini API: The texts and URLs you submit are sent to the Gemini API for basic analysis. This data is not used by Google to train its public models.
  • ARIA OS (AI Agent): The internal artificial intelligence that acts as an overlay. It processes raw data to extract highly relevant and calibrated scripts, etc.
  • Cakenews Labs: Mmedia Universe entity responsible for the technical architecture development of Aria OS. Its teams exchange data with the ARIA OS agent, notably to power and optimize the third-party news platform Cakenews.

5. Cookie Policy

The Aria OS Application uses cookies and similar technologies (Local Storage, Session Storage) in a very limited way and only for strictly necessary technical reasons:

  • Authentication Cookies (Firebase Auth): Allow keeping your session active without having to reconnect at each visit.
  • Local Preferences: Saving your theme (light/dark).

No advertising or third-party tracking cookies (type Google Analytics or Meta Pixel) are used on this Application. Consequently, no cookie consent banner is required under regulations (GDPR), technical cookies being exempt from prior consent.

6. Your Rights

In accordance with applicable data protection regulations, you have a right of access, rectification, erasure, and portability of your data. To exercise these rights, you can use the deletion options in the Application or contact us at the number indicated in the Legal Mentions.
